summary: End-to-end testing of CivicConnect MVP complete. All 25 backend pytest tests passed (auth, issues, uploads, RBAC, notifications, stats, leaderboard, community, contact). Frontend critical flows (citizen+admin login, report creation, track, notifications, leaderboard, community, contact, analytics, admin Manage modal status update, RBAC redirect, mobile hamburger) all working with the expected data-testids. App is healthy and meets the stated MVP scope.

minor.issue: allow_credentials=True combined with allow_origins=['*'] is invalid per CORS spec; many browsers (and Starlette) will reject credentialed cross-origin requests. Replace '*' with explicit FRONTEND_URL list.

Block logic 'existing_admins >= 1 and not (existing_admins == 1 and seeded)' allows creating a 2nd admin once the seeded one exists, but never blocks future calls if seeded admin is later removed. Consider explicit allow-only-when-no-non-seeded-admin (matches /admin-available).
Reset token is generated and logged but no /api/auth/reset-password endpoint exists to consume it - dead flow.

issues: Legend swatch colours are inverted vs slices: legend shows green=Pending/blue=Resolved while donut renders blue=Pending/green=Resolved. Minor confusion.
Visible on every authenticated page including dashboards - acceptable but consider hiding on /admin and /dashboard for cleaner control room look.

action_items: (Optional) Tighten CORS: replace allow_origins=['*'] with explicit list when allow_credentials=True.
(Optional) Implement /api/auth/reset-password to consume the password-reset token, or remove the forgot-password code path.
(Optional) Fix Analytics Pie chart colour/legend mapping consistency.
(Optional) Strengthen admin-register guard logic for edge cases.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is ~540 lines and growing; consider splitting into routers (auth, issues, uploads, admin, notifications) for maintainability.
CORS misconfiguration: allow_credentials=True + allow_origins=['*'] is spec-invalid.
No rate limiting on /api/auth/login - brute force possible.
JWT stored in localStorage on the client (cc_token) in addition to httpOnly cookie - exposes XSS surface; consider using only the cookie for app session.
Object storage init failure raises 500 on every upload until restart - add lazy retry/circuit breaker.
Issues list endpoint returns up to 500 docs without pagination - will not scale.
compute_badge thresholds are hard-coded both in backend and likely UI - centralize.
test_credentials: Citizen: citizen@civicconnect.in / Citizen@123; Admin: admin@civicconnect.in / Admin@123; Admin secret: CIVIC-ADMIN-2026 (all pre-seeded)
seed_data_creation: Tests created several TEST_-prefixed citizens and 3 issues (CC-2026-00001..00003) plus contact messages. Not cleaned up; harmless for further testing.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend pytest suite at /app/backend/tests/test_civicconnect.py covers full happy path + RBAC + 25 cases. Re-run: pytest /app/backend/tests/ -v. Frontend uses BACKEND_URL from REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL. Issue counter is in db.counters with _id='issue_2026'; first call after fresh DB yields CC-2026-00001. data-testids enumerated in original review_request all confirmed present. Admin Manage modal selectors verified working (modal-status/officer/remark/save). RBAC redirect from /admin to / for citizen confirmed. Mobile hamburger uses default aria-label and works at 390px.
